title: Binary Search, Selection, and Boundary Finding · Complexity Checkpoint
summary: Locate inventory thresholds and percentiles without off-by-one failures. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a boundary-safe search and selection toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a precise candidate interval and prove each binary-search update shrinks it.
Find first true, last false, lower bound, and upper bound under duplicate values.
Binary-search an answer space only after proving a monotone feasibility predicate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
